articles.title: POCO X8 Pro Max review: High-end performance at a competitive price
articles.description: POCO X8 Pro Max targets performance enthusiasts with its potent chipset and substantial battery, offering flagship-like speed at a mid-range price. While excelling in gaming and heavy tasks with effective cooling, its camera and software experience present trade-offs.

articles.title: Honda ZR-V India Launch soon: Five things to know
articles.description: There are currently no details on which engine the Honda ZR-V will be offered with in India. It’s expected to get a 2.0-litre, 4-cylinder, NA petrol engine paired with an e-motor. It puts out 180 hp & 240 nm torque. In terms of size, the Honda ZR-V measures about 4,568 mm in length, 1,840 mm in width and 1,620 mm in height, with a wheelbase of around 2,655-2,657 mm.

articles.description: WhatsApp is discontinuing avatars, removing creation, editing and profile support. The phased rollout also takes avatar tools out of settings, chats and the keyboard, while saved stickers remain available.

articles.title: Skyroot Vikram-1 rocket is raring to soar high
articles.description: Skyroot Aerospace is on the cusp of launching India's first privately built orbital rocket, Vikram-1, from Sriharikota within weeks. This crucial test flight aims to gather data for future commercial missions, carrying diverse payloads. Leveraging ISRO's infrastructure, a successful launch will position Skyroot as a key player in India's burgeoning private space sector, attracting global satellite operators.

articles.title: D2C brands tweak packaging as West Asia conflict pushes up costs
articles.description: Input cost hikes, fueled by the West Asia conflict, are forcing direct-to-consumer brands to adapt. Companies are shifting packaging, introducing larger or higher-priced variants, and accelerating supply chain localization to manage surging costs and protect margins. This strategic reevaluation impacts pricing and product offerings across various consumer segments.

articles.title: Tata Electronics aims to be $30 billion business with fab play: CEO & MD Randhir Thakur
articles.description: Tata Electronics is rapidly expanding its semiconductor and electronics manufacturing operations. The company has achieved significant revenue growth and is now profitable. With ambitious plans, it aims to become a $30 billion business in the next five years. Tata Electronics is building India's first semiconductor fab and an assembly and test unit, securing global customers and partners.

articles.title: EVs moving at faster speed in premium lane
articles.description: India's electric car market is seeing growth in premium segments. Carmakers are launching more expensive models, with sales in the ₹20-30 lakh bracket rising significantly. The share of cars below ₹10 lakh has decreased. Luxury EV demand is also strong. This trend suggests the market is expanding upwards, potentially limiting mass adoption.

articles.title: IT's revenue per employee up in productivity win
articles.description: India's leading IT firms are seeing revenue per employee rise. This indicates a shift towards AI and automation driving productivity. Companies like Tata Consultancy Services, Infosys, HCLTech, and Tech Mahindra reported growth. This trend suggests a move away from headcount-based revenue models. The industry is adapting to a new era of efficiency and value creation.

articles.title: MEA hails GalaxEye's Mission Drishti, a milestone for India's space
articles.description: The MEA hailed GalaxEye's Mission Drishti, a milestone for India's space innovation. The world's first OptoSAR satellite reflects the growing capabilities of India's private space sector and marks a major step in the country's space journey.

articles.title: Taiwan, Eswatini boost ties with energy security, innovation projects
articles.description: Taiwan's President Lai Ching-te visited Eswatini, its sole African ally, to bolster ties through projects like an industrial park and oil reserve. The visit drew sharp condemnation from China, which called the trip 'despicable'.

articles.title: ADB will help finance $70 bn worth of projects to improve electricity-sharing, internet access across Asia-Pacific by 2035
articles.description: The Asian Development Bank plans to back $70 billion in energy and digital infrastructure projects across Asia-Pacific by 2035 to improve connectivity and access. Under its power grid initiative, it aims to mobilise $50 billion to link cross-border electricity networks, support renewable energy, and expand transmission infrastructure.

articles.title: Why you need a digital spring cleaning (and how Incogni makes it effortless)
articles.description: As winter finally thaws and the weather warms up, we’re all tackling our annual spring cleaning routines. We declutter our closets and deep-clean our homes, yet we often overlook our digital lives. Personal information like your home address, phone number, date of birth, and financial history are all scattered across the internet. It’s actively being collected and sold by invisible data brokers. Spring is the time to take out the digital trash and reclaim your privacy with Incogni , and 9to5Mac readers can save 55% right now. more...

articles.title: How to lock a WhatsApp chat: A step-by-step guide
articles.description: WhatsApp now allows users to lock individual chats for enhanced privacy. Locked conversations are moved to a hidden folder, accessible only with your phone's passcode, fingerprint, Face ID, or a custom secret code. Notifications for these chats are also anonymized, ensuring your private conversations remain secure.

articles.title: Mission Drishti: India's largest private satellite boosts space power
articles.description: GalaxEye's Mission Drishti, India's largest privately built satellite, has successfully launched, marking a new era for India's space sector. The 190kg spacecraft features world-first OptoSAR technology for all-weather Earth observation.

articles.title: Rajnath Singh to open North Tech Symposium in Prayagraj on Monday
articles.description: Defence Minister Rajnath Singh will inaugurate the three-day North Tech Symposium in Prayagraj. The event, themed 'Raksha Triveni Sangam', is organised by the Indian Army and SIDM to showcase indigenous technologies for operational challenges.

articles.title: Elon Musk ‘agrees’ to the AI training practice that Anthropic complained to White House against China
articles.description: Elon Musk testified that his AI startup, xAI, has used OpenAI's models for training, admitting "partly" when questioned. This practice, known as model distillation, allows one AI to train another and is a growing concern in the industry regarding intellectual property and ethical boundaries.
